primary_reason: This state-transition microsimulation compares tuberculosis screening and preventive-treatment timing strategies for people initiating HIV care in South Africa.
This adapted microsimulation projects the epidemiologic and economic effects of alternative hepatitis C screening and treatment scale-up strategies in Moldova.
This study develops a spatial microsimulation approach that synthesizes disparate data into a synthetic population for small-area estimation of standard HIV indicators.
This adolescent HIV microsimulation compares in-person care, telehealth, and pandemic-related care interruption scenarios over ten years.
This individual-level microsimulation evaluates the cost-utility of nirmatrelvir and ritonavir for COVID-19 in a vaccinated Brazilian population.
This HIV microsimulation contrasts equal and equity-centered care-continuum improvements to project racial differences in life expectancy gains.
The longitudinal microsimulation models household structure, demographic change, and age-specific infectious-disease transmission mechanisms.
This natural-history microsimulation compares targeted and treat-all eligibility algorithms for hepatitis B therapy in The Gambia.
This patient-level simulation uses sepsis survival data to compare the costs and outcomes of albumin and crystalloid resuscitation.
This microsimulation concerns hereditary breast and ovarian cancer testing and identifies no concrete transfer route to infectious-disease epidemiology.
The decision-tree microsimulation estimates economically justifiable prophylaxis prices using costs, outcomes, and willingness-to-pay thresholds.
This HIV microsimulation compares observed and guideline-concordant care to project life expectancy across gender and sexual-orientation groups in Brazil.
The Markov microsimulation compares combined HPV vaccination and cervical screening strategies with probabilistic uncertainty analysis.
The microsimulation compares COVID-19 booster frequencies across risk groups while representing waning immunity and variant emergence.
The microsimulation projects Rwanda's HCV epidemic and compares elimination strategies, costs, mortality, and feasibility.
This agent-based microsimulation uses counterfactual program removal and Theil indices to estimate the Ryan White program's effects on HIV-care equity.
This adolescent-specific state-transition model compares life expectancy under current and ideal care for youth with perinatal or nonperinatal HIV.
The trial-based microsimulation compares costs and pathogen detection under near-patient and standard STI testing strategies.
The microsimulation represents HPV transmission and cancer natural history to compare elimination policy scenarios.
This microsimulation evaluates genetic testing and targeted therapy for ovarian and breast cancer without a concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This COVID-19 transmission microsimulation supports rapid stakeholder selection of prevention and implementation components for a hybrid randomized trial.
This microsimulation integrates smoking mortality with HIV care disengagement and viral suppression to estimate gains from smoking cessation in South Africa.
This microsimulation compares needle and syringe program scenarios for recurrent injection-related infections, mortality, service use, and cost-effectiveness.
This seven-state microsimulation compares family-based and traditional Helicobacter pylori screen-and-treat strategies using Chinese real-world data.
This individual-based state-transition model compares surveillance intervals and age ranges for hepatocellular carcinoma after hepatitis B virological remission.
This scoping review characterizes digital-twin types, development techniques, applications, and data sources across health fields including infectious diseases.
The citation identifies an erratum to an infection-related microsimulation study, but no correction text is supplied to determine whether it contains substantive methodological content.
This cervical-cancer microsimulation evaluates 198 computer-assisted cytology scenarios defined by HPV-program test performance and cost.
This surveillance-calibrated microsimulation evaluates vaccination timing, allocation, and sexual-contact changes during the Los Angeles County mpox outbreak.
This adapted microsimulation compares screen-and-treat and triage strategies for high-risk HPV while quantifying overtreatment, missed cancers, and follow-up effects in Uganda.
This dynamic agent-based model estimates how pneumococcal and Haemophilus influenzae type B vaccination changes antibiotic consumption and inequities in India.
This lifetime microsimulation compares hepatitis C fibrosis-staging strategies while explicitly modeling loss to follow-up and test-and-treat alternatives.
This microsimulation balances tuberculosis-preventive treatment benefits against maternal and fetal harms for pregnant people with HIV in South Africa.
This synthetic-population study evaluates and recalibrates orphanhood-based adult-mortality estimation methods for populations affected by HIV epidemics.
This trial microsimulation decomposes HIV-prevention efficacy dilution into risk heterogeneity, nonadherence, arm imbalance, and anal intercourse.
This microsimulation combines HIV-care dynamics with competing mortality to project the effects of smoking cessation on lung cancer and stroke among people with HIV.
This validated family-linked microsimulation addresses hereditary breast and ovarian cancer testing without a concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The study uses individual-level natural-history microsimulation to evaluate alternative HPV-related cancer screening strategies.
This Markov microsimulation tests how high-risk HPV prevalence and screening uptake alter the cost-effectiveness of self-sampling and visual inspection in Bangladesh.
This simulation-based method study compares fixed, dynamic, and hybrid time representations and demonstrates bias mitigation for epidemic and resource-constraint models.
This microsimulation evaluates Lynch syndrome cascade testing and colorectal-cancer surveillance without a concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This vessel-and-crew microsimulation compares quarantine and predeparture testing strategies for controlling respiratory-pandemic outbreaks at ports.
This mechanistic study develops an R package and behavioral-state microsimulation framework for resource-driven mosquito dispersal and pathogen-transmission modeling.
This HIV-progression and care microsimulation evaluates how adherence to viral-load monitoring guidelines affects vertical transmission in Kenya.
This hybrid HPV microsimulation compares 309 cervical-screening strategies using a composite harms-to-cancers-prevented criterion and vaccination stratification.
This cohort-recalibrated HPV microsimulation evaluates whether Dutch cervical-screening start and exit ages should change across vaccinated and unvaccinated cohorts.
This review compares simulation-based gastric-cancer screening strategies and explains how Helicobacter pylori risk stratification and screening intervals affect cost-effectiveness.
This microsimulation compares anal-cancer screening tests, triage rules, starting ages, and intervals for men with HIV while quantifying benefits, harms, and costs.
This cervical-cancer microsimulation compares HPV-status-dependent screening exit ages and triage methods in the Netherlands.
This Markov microsimulation compares dual-stain and cytology reflex testing after high-risk HPV genotyping in Singapore's screening program.
This microsimulation introduces progressive lung damage and post-tuberculosis sequelae and quantifies biases from omitting them in conventional burden models.
This patient-level microsimulation estimates the infection and cost effects of universal hypochlorous-acid and mupirocin decolonization for MRSA in burn units.
This HIV microsimulation compares immediate, delayed, trial-dependent, and incomplete implementation of long-acting antiretroviral therapy for persistent viremia.
This validated HIV microsimulation compares continued treatment, immediate switching, and resistance-guided switching after persistent virological nonsuppression in South Africa.
This HPV microsimulation identifies attendance, switching, follow-up, and kit-waste thresholds at which cervical self-sampling remains effective and cost-effective.
This Canadian HPV microsimulation compares one-time, population-phased, and age-phased rollout of primary HPV screening and projects colposcopy demand.
The study uses microsimulation to estimate vaccine-associated financial risk protection across wealth groups.
This linked population, epidemic, economic, and education simulation compares COVID-19 closure policies under competing health, poverty, GDP, and welfare objectives.
This tobacco-tax microsimulation projects noninfectious disease and economic outcomes without a concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The microsimulation evaluates antiretroviral strategies for vertical transmission, survival, costs, and cost-effectiveness.
This trial-informed Monte Carlo microsimulation evaluates enhanced surgical-site infection prevention across three surgery types, horizons, and utility valuations.
This adolescent HIV microsimulation projects lifetime care costs and identifies antiretroviral prices and low-CD4 care as principal cost drivers.
This state-transition microsimulation develops and internally validates a risk-prediction model for targeting HPV vaccination to men at greatest oral HPV16 risk.
This microsimulation quantifies life-years, quality-adjusted life-years, and costs attributable to frailty and falls among virally suppressed people with HIV.
This country-calibrated HPV microsimulation compares single-dose and two-dose vaccination schedules under supply constraints in Gavi-supported countries.
This calibrated Monte Carlo microsimulation estimates how an overdose prevention center would affect injection-related infections, hospitalizations, mortality, and costs.
This sociodemographically stratified microsimulation combines resistance surveillance, resource use, expenditures, and labor data to estimate Ghana's societal AMR burden.
This Chilean natural-history microsimulation compares HPV genotyping with dual-stain or cytology reflex testing against cytology-based cervical screening.
This tuberculosis trial simulation demonstrates how disaggregated clinical and cost outcomes can expose tradeoffs hidden by conventional aggregated value-of-information analysis.
This microsimulation models depressive symptoms under hypothetical lockdowns but does not model infection outcomes or establish another concrete infectious-disease transfer route.

This systematic review compares subgroup definitions, model structures, equity indices, quality, and implementation challenges in distributional cost-effectiveness studies including infectious-disease applications.
The study compares three calibrated simulation models to identify cost-effective screening strategies across vaccination-defined cohorts.
This COVID-19 microsimulation compares observed vaccination with counterfactual removal of misinformation to estimate attributable cases, hospitalizations, deaths, and costs.
This HIV microsimulation estimates clinical effects and cost thresholds for adding long-acting pre-exposure prophylaxis for high-vulnerability populations in Brazil.
This study develops, tests, and calibrates a mother-infant dyad microsimulation of cytomegalovirus acquisition, vertical transmission, and congenital outcomes.
This nationwide cohort study uses geospatial microsimulation to translate changing pediatric infection pressure into febrile-seizure incidence, admissions, and costs.
This HIV aging simulation projects Medicare enrollment and costs while evaluating savings under alternative antiretroviral price scenarios.
This dynamic microsimulation integrates a large Brazilian cohort with demographic and economic changes to estimate how income mobility policies could alter AIDS incidence.
This Markov microsimulation compares biomarker-based and ultrasound-based hepatocellular-carcinoma surveillance for people with chronic hepatitis B or cirrhosis in Hong Kong.
